name: DEV.to MCP
what_it_does: MCP server by prominent DEV.to community member nickytonline enabling AI agents to interact with the DEV.to developer blogging platform — reading articles, searching posts, accessing user profiles, querying trending content, and managing blog posts through the DEV.to API.
use_cases: Searching and reading technical articles on DEV.to from research agents
Discovering trending developer content from content curation agents
Managing DEV.to blog posts and drafts from content creation agents
Gathering developer community insights from market research agents
not_for: General web search (DEV.to is specific to developer-authored technical content)
Medium, Hashnode, or other blogging platforms (DEV.to-specific API)
best_when: An agent workflow needs to research or curate developer-written technical content from DEV.to — particularly for discovering tutorials, opinions, and community knowledge on programming topics.
avoid_when: You need broader technical documentation (use GitHub docs or official sources) or general web search — DEV.to is community-generated content with variable quality.

auth.notes: DEV.to API key required for write operations. Read operations may work without authentication. Get API key from dev.to/settings/extensions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: DEV.to API is free with rate limits; MCP server is free
pricing.notes: Free open source from nickytonline. DEV.to API is free with rate limiting.

performance.rate_limits: DEV.to API rate limits apply: typically 30 requests/second

known_agent_gotchas: DEV.to content quality varies significantly — not authoritative like official documentation
API rate limits (30 req/sec) can constrain bulk content operations
From nickytonline, a well-known DEV.to contributor — community-trusted but not official DEV.to MCP
Article content may be outdated — check publication dates for technical accuracy
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS. API key for write operations. DEV.to content is public. API key controls article publishing — keep secure. No sensitive personal data exposed beyond public DEV.to profiles.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. API key for write access. Public content access requires no auth. API key can publish articles — protect from exposure.
